title: BGLS step: convexity does not directly give Hessian PSD (need C^2 at the point)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
In Corollary (cor:psd) the conclusion $\sum_i\gamma_i H^{(i)}\succeq 0$ is obtained from the Abel summation $L=\sum_{k<n}(c_k-c_{k+1})\sigma_k+c_n\sigma_n$ together with the claim 'Hess$\,\sigma_k\succeq0$ by BGLS.'

BGLS supplies only the **convexity** of $\sigma_k=\sum_{i\le k}\lambda_i$. Convexity is a global statement and does NOT by itself yield a PSD Hessian at a specific point: that requires $\sigma_k$ to be twice differentiable there. The implication actually needed is
$$\sigma_k\text{ convex and }C^2\text{ at }(\alpha,\beta)\ \Longrightarrow\ \operatorname{Hess}\sigma_k(\alpha,\beta)\succeq0.$$
The twice-differentiability holds because at simple roots each $\gamma_i$ (hence each $\sigma_k$) is real-analytic (implicit function theorem applied to $\partial_x f\neq0$), but the solution never states this, never invokes the 'convex+$C^2$ ⇒ Hessian PSD' lemma, and never ties the differentiability to the simple-rootedness guaranteed (post-reduction) by $T_\epsilon^2$ and Nuij. The logical chain 'BGLS ⇒ Hess$\,\sigma_k\succeq0$' is therefore incomplete as written.

Fix: state a regularity lemma (analyticity of the ordered root map on the simple-root locus) and the standard convex-analysis fact, applied only inside $U=\{p,q,p\boxplus q\text{ all simple}\}$.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
**Resolved.** The gap was that Corollary (cor:psd) inferred $\operatorname{Hess}\sigma_k(z_0)\succeq0$ from BGLS *convexity* alone, without (a) the convex+$C^2$⇒PSD principle and (b) twice-differentiability of $\sigma_k$ at the evaluation point. I made both explicit and tied the regularity to the simple-root reduction.

**What I added / changed in solution.tex:**

1. **Lemma (lem:convC2): convex $+$ $C^2$ at a point $\Rightarrow$ Hessian PSD.** Full proof via the one-dimensional restriction $\phi(t)=g(z_0+th)$: convexity gives the nonnegative symmetric second difference, and the second-order Taylor expansion (the hypothesis) shows this quotient $\to\phi''(0)=h^\ast\operatorname{Hess}g(z_0)h$. So $\operatorname{Hess}g(z_0)\succeq0$. This is the standard convex-analysis fact, now stated and proved rather than asserted.

2. **Lemma (lem:reg): real-analyticity of the ordered hyperbolic eigenvalues on the simple-spectrum locus.** Proof via the real-analytic implicit function theorem applied to $F(z,t)=f(z-te)$ at a *simple* root: $\partial_t F(z_0,\lambda_i(z_0))=g_0'(\lambda_i(z_0))\neq0$ because $g_0(s)=f(z_0-se)$ has simple zeros. The local analytic root functions are forced (by ordering/continuity on disjoint neighbourhoods) to coincide with the $\lambda_i$; hence each $\sigma_k=\sum_{i\le k}\lambda_i$ is real-analytic, in particular $C^2$, near $z_0$.

3. **Explicit combination** of Theorem (BGLS) $+$ lem:reg $+$ lem:convC2, stated as the precise implication used: *simple spectrum at $z_0$ $\Rightarrow$ $\operatorname{Hess}\sigma_k(z_0)\succeq0$*, recorded as a labelled equation (eq:Hesssigmak-PSD).

4. **Localization to the simple-spectrum point.** The Corollary now evaluates everything at $z_0=(0,\alpha,\beta)$, where, by the eigenvalue identity $\lambda_i(z)=x-\rho_{n+1-i}(a,b)$, the spectrum is $\{-\gamma_{n+1-i}\}$ — pairwise distinct exactly because $p\boxplus q$ has simple roots after the $T_\epsilon^2$/Nuij reduction (Lemma simple). The Abel-summation Hessian identity is asserted only at $z_0$ (where $L$ is $C^2$), and the restricted-Hessian/principal-submatrix step is done at $z_0$.

5. **Standing-assumptions lemma in Step 1 (lem:rootreg):** ties the simple-rootedness (post-reduction) to smoothness of the ordered root map at $(\alpha,\beta)$ via lem:reg, which is what makes the Jacobian and the Hessians $H^{(i)}$ used in Lemma (hess) and the Corollary exist classically in the first place.

6. **Removed the hand-wavy "distributional Hessian" remark**; we never use a distributional/everywhere statement. The final parenthetical now states plainly that the whole argument lives at the single simple-spectrum point $z_0$, which is precisely where the $H^{(i)}$ are defined and where convex$\Rightarrow$PSD is licensed.

**Checks:** all `\ref`/`\eqref` resolve (no missing/duplicate labels), braces/environments balanced. Numerically verified for $n=3$ that $\sum_j\gamma_j H^{(j)}\succeq0$ with $\gamma$ decreasing (eigenvalues $\ge0$ up to FD error), confirming the corollary's conclusion. (No LaTeX toolchain available in the sandbox, so I could not run pdflatex.)

The logical chain 'BGLS $\Rightarrow$ $\operatorname{Hess}\sigma_k\succeq0$' is now complete: convexity (BGLS) + $C^2$ (lem:reg, IFT on the simple-root locus) $\Rightarrow$ PSD (lem:convC2), applied only at the simple-spectrum point.
